title: hieracium umbellatum subsp. brevifolioides
intro: Hieracium umbellatum subsp. brevifolioides, also known as hawkweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the dandelion-like plants in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The medicinal properties of this plant are primarily found in its leaves and stems, which contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hieracium umbellatum subsp. brevifolioides include its potential use in treating digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
